title: IDF/USAF Day 34 Strikes Continue Across Iran; Trump: 'War Very Much Complete'
detail: US and Israeli forces continued Day 34 strike operations across Iran on April 2. Trump publicly stated he believed 'there is practically nothing left to hit' and that 'the war is very much complete,' while insisting on unconditional surrender as the only acceptable end. DefSec Hegseth confirmed strike operations continued nonetheless, with US forces maintaining pressure to force Hormuz reopening before the April 6 deadline. Al Jazeera reported the Day 34 strikes included continued targeting of IRGC command nodes, remaining missile storage facilities, and military infrastructure in western Iran. CENTCOM noted cumulative strike totals exceeded 11,000+ targets struck since February 28.

title: IDF Strikes Beirut, Kills Senior Hezbollah Commander and 7 Civilians
detail: Israeli airstrikes hit Beirut on April 2, killing a senior Hezbollah commander and at least 7 civilians according to Lebanon's Ministry of Health. The strike in southern Beirut's Dahiyeh suburb targeted a building used by Hezbollah's senior military leadership. Lebanon's Ministry of Health confirmed civilian casualties including women and children among the dead. The Lebanese government — which had already formally moved to ban Hezbollah's military wing — condemned the civilian deaths. The strike comes amid Hezbollah's continued rocket and missile barrages, which have exceeded 1,400 attack waves since March 2. IDF confirmed targeting a 'senior Hezbollah commander responsible for rocket operations against northern Israel.'

media.caption: Aftermath of IDF strike on Beirut Dahiyeh suburb; at least 7 civilians killed alongside senior Hezbollah commander

title: The Intercept Alleges Pentagon Casualty Cover-Up: ~750 US Troops Killed or Wounded
detail: The Intercept published an investigative report on April 1-2 alleging the Pentagon is engaged in a systematic cover-up of US military casualties, with approximately 750 US troops killed or wounded since the Iran war began — far exceeding the 13 KIA officially confirmed by the Pentagon. The report alleged that Secretary of Defense Hegseth ordered strict restrictions on casualty reporting and media embeds. The Pentagon denied the allegations, calling the figures 'completely fabricated.' The report referenced anonymous service members, veterans advocacy groups, and hospital manifests from US military facilities in the theater. The discrepancy between the official 13 KIA figure and the alleged ~750 total casualty figure drew significant domestic US political backlash, particularly from Congressional Democrats requesting an immediate Inspector General investigation.

title: 4 Days Before April 6 Hormuz Deadline — Iran Shows No Sign of Opening Strait
detail: With four days remaining before Trump's April 6 deadline for full Hormuz reopening, Iran showed no signs of compliance as of April 2. The IRGC maintained its 'Tehran toll booth' selective passage regime, allowing only ships flagged by China, Russia, India, Iraq, Pakistan, Malaysia, and Thailand to transit — at a cost of several million dollars per vessel. The IEA reiterated that the global buffer from SPR releases would expire by mid-April, triggering what analysts are calling the 'April oil cliff.' Trump had publicly stated on March 27 he would strike Iranian power plants if Hormuz was not fully open by April 6. The 35-nation London conference failed to produce a binding agreement. Iran's 5 conditions for a permanent war end — including Hormuz sovereignty recognition — remained incompatible with Trump's unconditional-surrender stance.

title: HRANA Day 33-34 Report: 8 Civilians Killed in 24 Hours; Cumulative Dead Near 7,000
detail: HRANA (Human Rights Activists News Agency) released Day 33-34 casualty figures on April 2, reporting 8 Iranian civilians killed and 14 injured in a single 24-hour period from ongoing strikes. HRANA's cumulative figure reached approximately 3,460+ killed including 1,551 civilians and 244 children. Iran International separately estimated 4,700+ IRGC and security forces killed as of April 1. Hengaw's last published figure (March 25) was 6,530+ total, including 640 confirmed civilians. The wide variance between organizations reflects the near-total internet blackout inside Iran (still at approximately 1% connectivity, 840+ hours cumulative), which severely limits independent verification. The WHO documented 18 hospitals struck and 190+ health/medical centers hit since February 28.
